data.title: T. Rowe Price launches Innovation Leaders ETF, targeting disruptive companies in tech, healthcare and finance sectors.
data.description: The recently launched Innovation Leaders ETF presents an opportunity for investors to tap into groundbreaking companies in sectors such as technology, healthcare, and financials. On January 29, 2026, T. Rowe Price, a prominent global investment management company with a focus on retirement, revealed the incorporation of this ETF into its offerings.

data.title: Knowtion Health gets top ratings in KLAS First Look Report for reducing denials & improving patient engagement. A grades across all key performance indicators.
data.description: New data reveals that Knowtion Health in Boca Raton, Florida, is effectively tackling Coordination of Benefit denials by improving patient engagement. The survey showcased their success in this area, as the healthcare revenue cycle company received A grades across all key performance indicators. This achievement demonstrates their dedication to reducing denials and enhancing the coordination of benefits process. The results of the survey, conducted on Jan. 29, 2026, highlight the positive impact of Knowtion Health's strategies on improving revenue cycle efficiency and ensuring better outcomes for patients.

data.title: Replay Sports Cards signed lease for new Indiana location in Seymour. America's first national sports card franchise.
data.description: A sports card shop franchise called Replay Sports Cards ("Replay"), which is focused on serving the community, is set to open soon in Seymour, Indiana. The franchise, considered America's first national franchise solely dedicated to the sports card hobby, recently secured a lease for its new location in Seymour. The announcement was made on January 29, 2026.

data.title: Peoples Financial Corp reported Q4 2025 net income decreased to $716,000, with total interest income at $6,569,000 and total interest expense at $1,930,000. For the year ended 2025, net income decreased to $3,911,000 from $21,703,000 in 2024. Shareholders' equity increased to $100,667,000 from $90,001,000 in 2024. Total deposits decreased to $604,429,000 from $720,730,000 in 2024 due to loss of large public fund deposits. The Bank's leverage ratio stood at 15.76% as of December 31, 2025. Annual meeting of shareholders set for April 22, 2026. Earnings, interest income, interest expense, deposits, and equity changing significantly every quarter.
data.description: Peoples Financial Corporation reported their fourth-quarter earnings for the period ending December 31, 2025. The net income decreased to $716,000 from $1,528,000 in the fourth quarter of 2024. Earnings per weighted average common share were $0.16 in Q4 2025, down from $0.33 in Q4 2024. The Company also announced an extension of their stock repurchase program with no expiration date, allowing for the repurchase of up to $750,000 or 40,000 shares of common stock. Their 2026 annual meeting of shareholders is scheduled for April 22, 2026. Total interest income decreased to $6,569,000 in Q4 2025 compared to $7,640,000 in Q4 2024, while total interest expense decreased to $1,930,000 from $2,064,000 during the same periods. For the year ended 2025, net income declined to $3,911,000 from $21,703,000 in 2024, with earnings per weighted average common share at $0.85 in 2025 and $4.66 in 2024. Shareholders' equity grew to $100,667,000 by December 31, 2025, from $90,001,000 in 2024. The Bank improved its leverage ratio, remaining strong at 15.76% as of December 31, 2025. Additionally, total deposits decreased to $604,429,000 by the end of 2025 from $720,730,000 in 2024, primarily due to the loss of large public fund deposits. The Company remains well-capitalized and emphasizes maintaining high-quality assets and strong liquidity. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Circle reveals plan for 2026: Arc blockchain mainnet launch, expanded USDC infrastructure, and scaling enterprise payment network.
data.description: Information has been released about Circle's product roadmap for 2026, outlining key plans such as the launch of the Arc blockchain mainnet, expansion of the USDC infrastructure, and scaling of the enterprise payment network. The roadmap indicates a significant focus on advancing blockchain technology and increasing capabilities for USDC, along with enhancing the efficiency and reach of the payment network for businesses. This strategic roadmap sets a clear direction for the company's development and growth, with a strong emphasis on innovation and expansion in the coming years.

data.title: Porter Airlines expands summer schedule with new destinations and routes, adding flights including Nashville and Boston.Characters: 111
data.description: Porter Airlines has revealed its summer schedule for 2026, featuring a host of new destinations, routes, increased frequencies, and the maintenance of favored winter markets. Notably, Porter’s U.S. network will see growth with fresh flights to Nashville International Airport (BNA) from Toronto City (YTZ). Additionally, Boston (BOS) will have new service from both Toronto Pearson (YYZ) and Montreal (YUL). In an effort to bolster domestic service, Porter is set to launch six additional routes connecting various cities through Toronto Pearson. This strategic expansion signifies Porter Airlines' commitment to enhancing its services and accessibility for travelers.

data.title: SEC Chair Paul Atkins is open to allowing crypto in 401(k) accounts, managing $12.5 trillion, under professional guidance. New guidance for tokenized stocks requires issuer approval. Private securities and private equity are first, with crypto to follow.
data.description: The Chairman of the Securities and Exchange Commission, Paul Atkins, has initiated the possibility of incorporating cryptocurrency into $12.5 trillion worth of 401(k) retirement accounts. He has concurrently introduced fresh guidelines regarding the synthetic tokenized stock market, mandating issuer approval for genuine equity tokens. Atkins informed CNBC that he believes the timing is appropriate for allowing cryptocurrency exposure in 401(k) plans, pointing out that numerous Americans currently possess digital assets in professionally administered pension funds. The focus is on the structure, as in pension funds, professionals determine the assets included in portfolios. The SEC aims to establish a similar framework for 401(k) plans, with trustees and fund managers deciding the inclusions rather than individual account holders. Atkins disclosed that the initial focus will be on private securities and private equity, which are already present in managed retirement accounts. He likened cryptocurrency to an alternative asset that follows a comparable trajectory.
